summary: Closed, unmerged draft PR attempting broad decomp/matching work across 71 files in camera, fighter, stage, item, library, game-mode, and menu code. Loaded evidence shows several placeholder functions replaced with C bodies, headers updated from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS, and matching-oriented tactics such as PAD_STACK, scoped pragmas, register locals, padded temporary structs, and a documented fake comma-operator match. Issue comments state the branch drifted too far to merge and was closed in favor of #2666.

key_files.role: Implemented Camera_8002C1A8 with pause-camera input handling, slot changes, zoom/move/rotate handling, and a padded stack_inputs local struct.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: camera.c Camera_8002C1A8 body replaces placeholder

key_files.role: Updated Camera_8002C1A8 prototype from unknown return/params to void(void).
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: camera.h UNK_RET Camera_8002C1A8(UNK_PARAMS) -> void Camera_8002C1A8(void)

key_files.role: Mixed matching cleanup and tactics: include ordering, direct call replacing casted function pointer, scoped dont_inline pragmas, PAD_STACK, register locals, semantic rename to ftCo_CapturePulledHi_Coll, and K&R signature cleanup.
Small matching edits including motion-state enum substitutions and retention of a documented fake comma-operator regalloc tactic.
Added duplicate placeholder comments for unresolved Mewtwo-copy special-N functions.
Implemented several Popo/Nana special-hi functions using temporary dat_attrs structs, partner lookup, motion-id range checks, and shared Vec3 position capture.
Largest changed stage file in slice; metadata indicates substantial zako generator matching work but loaded diff excerpt is truncated before details.
Static header updated alongside grzakogenerator work.
Large item matching edit; details not visible in loaded diff excerpt.
Large item matching edit; details not visible in loaded diff excerpt.
Large item matching edit; details not visible in loaded diff excerpt.
Large item matching edit; details not visible in loaded diff excerpt.
Large item matching edit; details not visible in loaded diff excerpt.
Item common header updates paired with item matching work.

what_changed: The PR changed 71 files with 2219 added and 249 deleted lines according to metadata.
Camera_8002C1A8 was converted from a placeholder to a C implementation, and its header prototype was updated to void(void).

ftCo_Attack100.c received many small matching-oriented edits: include reordering, formatting, function rename/prototype cleanup, scoped pragmas, PAD_STACK retention, and a K&R-style definition converted to a prototype-style definition.
ftKb_SpecialN.c changed Kirby inhale/eat motion-state enum names in two calls and retained a comment identifying a fake comma-operator match for regalloc.
ftPp_SpecialS.c added temporary attribute structs and implemented air special-hi entry/physics functions that read dat_attrs offsets and query Nana/partner state.
Multiple item and stage files were changed substantially by metadata, but their exact code changes are not visible in the loaded truncated diff excerpt.
A bot report recorded 190 new matches but also very large regressions: 1637 broken matches, 919 regressions in unmatched items, and overall matched-code decrease of 11.05%.

Maintainer comments indicate the branch was not merged because the diff touched too many files, required manual conflict resolution, and later drift made it impossible to merge; it was closed in favor of #2666.
smart_moves: When Camera_8002C1A8 gained a concrete body, the matching header prototype was updated from unknown return/params to void(void), aligning with truthful-header practice.

The ftCo_Attack100.c edit kept an address-style/static helper name fn_800DAECC for an uncertain wrapper while using a semantic exported name ftCo_CapturePulledHi_Coll where the role was clearer in the diff.
Scoped pragma push/pop blocks were used around individual functions rather than applying a file-wide pragma, limiting blast radius if those tactics were necessary.
Temporary local structs in Camera_8002C1A8 and ftPp_SpecialS.c document stack or dat_attrs layout assumptions without claiming complete recovered global types.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Broad matching branches across many subsystems can become unreviewable or unmergeable when master drifts; this PR was closed because conflicts and branch drift made manual resolution impractical.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: ribbanya comment says diff touches too many files and conflicts need manual resolution
human_pr_text: jurrejelle comment says closing in favor of #2666 because drift made it impossible to merge

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a placeholder function is implemented, update the owning header signature in the same change if the body proves the signature.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: camera.h prototype changed to void Camera_8002C1A8(void)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Matching tactics such as PAD_STACK, register locals, fake comma operators, and dont_inline pragmas should be treated as narrow tactics requiring explicit verifier evidence; this PR shows their use but the loaded slice does not include objdiff proof for each tactic.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftCo_Attack100.c PAD_STACK and register locals
diff_excerpt: ftCo_Attack100.c scoped #pragma dont_inline on blocks
diff_excerpt: ftKb_SpecialN.c comment 'FAKE MATCH: comma operator required for regalloc'

naming_conventions.convention: Keep address-style fn_ names for helpers whose semantics are not proven, but use semantic names when the role is supported by local context.
examples: fn_800DAECC remains a static wrapper name
fn_800DAEEC declaration/body is replaced by ftCo_CapturePulledHi_Coll
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftCo_Attack100.c declaration and body rename

naming_conventions.convention: Temporary recovered structs may use subsystem/function-scoped names plus offset-like field names when full semantic field names are not proven.
examples: ftPpAirHiPhysAttrs with x8C, x90, xA8, xAC

naming_conventions.convention: Clear local role names appear in implemented functions when roles are known, such as fp, camera, slot, slot_ptr, move_x, move_y, rotate_x, rotate_y, stick_x, and substick_x.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Padded temporary stack struct to control local layout around CameraInputs.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: Camera_8002C1A8 defines struct { u8 pad[8]; CameraInputs inputs; } stack_inputs
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No per-function objdiff evidence is present in the loaded slice.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was retained in ftCo_ItemScopeRapid_Anim, ftCo_ItemScopeAirRapid_Anim, and fn_800DBED4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Global standards require explicit verifier evidence before keeping stack-padding tactics.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped #pragma dont_inline on was added around selected ftCo_Attack100.c functions.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on around fn_800DAA40
diff_excerpt: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on around fn_800DAD18
diff_excerpt: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on around fn_800DB5D8
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The slice shows tight scoping but not adjacent-function verification.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Register-qualified locals were used in ftCo_CapturePulledHi_Coll.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: register Fighter* temp_r31; register Fighter_GObj* temp_r30;
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Register steering is an exception under loaded standards and needs proof.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A fake comma operator was explicitly documented as required for regalloc in ftKb_SpecialAirHi3_Phys.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: 'FAKE MATCH: comma operator required for regalloc'
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Useful as historical evidence of a tactic, not as an accepted style rule.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Temporary dat_attrs structs with offset fields were introduced instead of raw pointer casts in Popo/Nana special-hi code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The fields remain offset-named, so this is partial type recovery rather than complete semantic naming.

review_feedback.feedback: Report showed 190 new matches but also broad regressions, including 1637 broken matches, 919 unmatched-item regressions, matched code down 11.05%, and matched data down 4.39%.
Initial idea of merging with git merge -s ours was abandoned because the branch diff against master touched too many files and conflicts needed manual resolution.
PR was closed in favor of #2666 because drift made it impossible to merge, and #2666 contained the only remaining matches from the branch not already in master.

follow_up_queries: PR #2666 zako matches carried over from #2238
decomp-dev bot report PR #2238 190 new matches 1637 broken matches
FAKE MATCH comma operator required for regalloc ftKb_SpecialAirHi3_Phys

diff_excerpt/raw_diff: visible hunks for camera.c, camera.h, ftCo_Attack100.c, ftKb_SpecialN.c, ftKb_SpecialNZd.c, ftPp_SpecialS.c
notes: The raw diff is truncated, so many changed files are known only from metadata and not from visible hunks.
There are no loaded review comments and no formal review records in the slice.
The PR was closed unmerged; do not treat its code shapes or tactics as accepted project style.
Bot report is useful evidence of broad match/regression impact, but not per-function proof for individual tactics.
Human comments explain closure and drift, but do not review individual code hunks.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Broad stale matching branches can become unmergeable
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2238 touched 71 files across many systems and was closed unmerged after maintainers stated the diff touched too many files, required manual conflict resolution, and later drift made it impossible to merge. Use this as historical evidence favoring smaller, focused matching PRs or early extraction of still-valid matches.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: jurrejelle closing in favor of #2666

source_update_candidates.title: Historical caution: do not promote matching tactics without verifier evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2238 contains PAD_STACK, scoped dont_inline pragmas, register locals, padded temporary structs, and a documented fake comma-operator regalloc tactic, but the loaded slice does not provide per-function objdiff proof. This supports the existing standard that such tactics need explicit verifier evidence and should remain narrow.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential supporting historical example for existing standards; not a new accepted rule.

source_update_candidates.title: PR #2238 touched grzakogenerator and many item/stage files but loaded diff is truncated
source_update_candidates.text: Changed-file metadata shows substantial edits to src/melee/gr/grzakogenerator.c (+332/-9), grzakogenerator headers, and many item/stage files, but the loaded diff excerpt does not expose their detailed hunks. Any path-specific lessons for those files require inspecting the full diff or PR #2666 carry-over.
source_update_candidates.reason: Route as a lookup candidate rather than asserting detailed path facts from truncated evidence.

rejection_notes: Do not promote PR #2238 as merged or accepted; metadata says merged_at is null and state is CLOSED.
Do not infer exact matches for every changed file from the title alone; many hunks are not visible in the loaded truncated diff.
Do not treat the bot report's 190 new matches as clean validation because the same report shows large broken-match and regression counts.
Do not convert the fake comma-operator, register locals, PAD_STACK, or pragmas into accepted style rules without per-symbol verification evidence.
Do not infer reviewer approval or intent; the slice contains no review comments and only issue comments about merge/drift handling.
